mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over 2008-2010.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion regimes, or the predictive power reverses to negative during the same regime over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 5-day forward returns over a 2-year rolling window, or accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 295.0, elevated inflation expectations force monetary tightening and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 295.0 are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the threshold fails to generate a significant negative Rank IC over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day returns with > 58% accuracy, or the interaction effect becomes insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0% alongside declining payrolls, the labor market enters a contractionary phase that suppresses earnings growth and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE > 4.0% and falling PAYEMS fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the signal produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.
